title: Orbits, 3D Deck, Liquid Type, Springs & States
description: Five live UI demos: CSS orbital motion paths, 3D card fan decks, SVG liquid text, linear() spring easing, and multi-state buttons.

drops.title: CSS offset-path Orbits
drops.description: 6 glowing orbs orbit along custom SVG paths using CSS offset-path — no JS animation loops.
drops.concept: CSS Motion Path (offset-path) lets you animate elements along arbitrary SVG paths purely in CSS. Each orb uses a different path — circle, figure-eight, tilted ellipse — all driven by @keyframes animating offset-distance from 0% to 100%. Hot because it removes the need for JS-driven position calculations for path-following animation.
drops.source: CSS Motion Path Level 1 spec / Chrome Developers

interaction.mouse: Buttons to pause, slow, fast, or reveal path traces
interaction.click: Click Pause/Play to halt all orbits

drops.title: 3D Card Fan Deck
drops.description: Three card decks that fan out in CSS 3D perspective on hover — lateral, arc, and depth-cascade variants.
drops.concept: Stacked card decks are a classic pattern getting a revival with CSS 3D transforms. Each card in a deck sits at a different z-index and gets a unique rotateY/rotateZ transform on :hover, creating a physical 'fan' feeling. Spring cubic-bezier overshoot easing makes them feel alive. Three layout variants showcase different creative directions — lateral spread, arc fan, and diagonal depth cascade.
drops.source: iOS card stack interaction / CSS 3D Transforms MDN

interaction.mouse: Hover each deck to fan cards out
interaction.touch: Touch and hold to trigger hover state

drops.title: SVG feTurbulence Liquid Text
drops.description: Text warps and melts using SVG feTurbulence + feDisplacementMap animated with SMIL — plus mouse-driven warp mode.
drops.concept: SVG filter primitives can distort rendered text in ways impossible with pure CSS. feTurbulence generates procedural noise; feDisplacementMap reads its R/G channels to offset each pixel of the source text. SMIL animate elements drive baseFrequency and scale attributes over time, making the text breathe and melt. Trending as part of the 'distortion typography' aesthetic in editorial design.
drops.source: SVG Filter Effects / MDN feTurbulence reference

interaction.mouse: Mouse Warp mode: move cursor to control distortion intensity
interaction.click: Buttons: Pause/Play, Mild, Wild, Mouse Warp toggle

drops.title: CSS linear() Spring Easing
drops.description: Spring physics baked into CSS timing functions using the new linear() easing function — no JS animation.
drops.concept: CSS linear() (shipped Chrome 113, Firefox 112) lets you specify arbitrary easing curves as a list of output values. Jake Archibald's spring easing generator samples a real spring simulation (mass-spring-damper) and encodes its output as a linear() value. This gives you realistic multi-bounce spring physics purely in CSS transitions, without any JS animation library. Three spring variants demonstrate snappy, bouncy, and slow-wobbly personalities.

interaction.mouse: Click Animate button or track itself to fire the transition
interaction.click: Reset button returns ball to start position

drops.title: Animated Multi-state Button
drops.description: Buttons cycle through idle → loading → success/error with SVG checkmark draws, spinners, and ripple effects.
drops.concept: Multi-state buttons replace separate status indicators with a single evolving control. The key technique: CSS class toggling drives opacity/transform transitions on layered elements (label, spinner, check icon), while SVG stroke-dashoffset reveals the checkmark path progressively. Three visual variants (pill gradient, outlined, skeuomorphic) show the pattern adapts to any design language. Widely used in payment flows, form submissions, and async actions.
drops.source: Emil Kowalski / Sonner toast library patterns

interaction.mouse: Click any button to cycle states
interaction.click: Button cycles: idle → loading → success or error → idle
